kept.text: @zerohedge Schrodinger's Strait - It's both open and closed. It's a Quantum mechanics thing..

جنگنده‌ها احتمالا در حال گشت‌زنی بر فراز تنگه هرمز هستند. ۵ هواپیمای سوخت‌رسان نیروی هوایی آمریکا بر فراز خلیج فارس در حال فعالیت‌اند، هم‌زمان با گزارش‌هایی درباره قوانین جدید درگیری که اجازه حمله به تهدیدات خصمانه را صادر می‌کنند.

نیروهای آمریکایی اکنون مجاز هستند تهدیدهای فوری علیه کشتی‌هایی را که از تنگه هرمز عبور می‌کنند، هدف قرار دهند. از جمله قایق‌های تندرو سپاه و مواضع موشکی ج‌ا.

El derbi de Schrodinger.
Sólo es derbi si gana el Getafe.

quoted_tweet.text:
NO es un derbi. El derbi del Getafe siempre ha sido, es y será contra el Leganés. Ni contra el Madrid, ni contra el Atleti, ni contra el Rayo, ni contra el Alcor, ni contra el Fuenla.

Si el Rayo quiere buscar un derbi, que siga buscando. Nosotros ya lo tenemos.

kept.text:
Vampire Hunter D vs. Alucard

Based on the strongest versions of these two. Vampire Hunter D (Light Novels) vs. Post-Schrodinger Alucard

​In my honest opinion: D wins with low difficulty and here's why I think so:

​1. The Schrodinger Paradox vs. D’s Conceptual Cutting. ​Alucard’s ultimate strength is his omnipresence. As long as he can observe himself, he exists everywhere and nowhere. He is effectively a living ghost that cannot be touched. ​The Problem is that D specializes in killing things that are immortal, non-existent, or conceptual.
​D has successfully slashed ghosts, nothingness, and the fabric of space-time. He even cut a black hole in half. D doesn’t need a physical body to hit. He can strike the essence of Alucard. If Alucard exists as a thought or a paradox, D’s sword which can cut through the laws of cause and effect will simply kill the paradox itself.

2. Alucard is arguably faster than the eye can see, but still physically bound by the world to some degree. Hunter D has reacted to and deflected attacks that move at the speed of light from across solar systems. He has moved so fast that he arrived at his destination before he even started moving breaking causality. ​Alucard wouldn't even realize the fight had started before it ended.

​3. ​D's Left Hand is a vacuum for supernatural energy. ​If Alucard tries to use soul-based attacks, blood manipulation, or telepathy, D's left hand simply eats it. It can also manipulate the elements and revive D if he should somehow die. Hunter D has access to the Akashic Records, which are essentially the source code of the universe.
​D can rewrite reality. He can change the past, present, or future by simply willing it. ​Alucard is a glitch in reality, but D is the System Administrator who can simply just hit delete.

Alucard is a nightmare for humans and lesser vampires. D is the nightmare of the beings who created the universe. Alucard doesn't have a single win con here in my opinion. D can bypass his immortality, outspeed his thoughts, and erase his soul from history. 

This is a battle of "I am everywhere" vs "I can kill the concept of everywhere." But this are just my take on how this insane battle will play out. 

I'd love to see what you guys make of this battle..

kept.text: عربستان و امارات  اکنون (به گفته منابع اسرائیلی) در حال فشار آوردن به آمریکا هستند تا جنگ با ج‌ا را از سر بگیرد.
تلاش برای بازگشایی تنگه هرمز یا به باز شدن مسیر منجر می‌شه یا به یک درگیری تمام عیار ختم می‌شه، با حضور آمریکا + اسرائیل + کشورهای عربی که تا الان در حاشیه ایستاده بودند.
مقام‌های اسرائیلی می‌گن آماده‌ان فورا عملیات نظامی علیه ج‌ا رو از سر بگیرن و منتظر تأیید واشنگتن هستن.
